DETAIL: Best: Based on WIM data that satisfies accuracy requirements for ASTM E1318-09 Type I WIM systems passing data reasonableness checks. Data include at least one of each day of the week in each month.
Agency ESAL based: No directly usable annual axle distribution. Agency ESAL estimate more reasonable than ESALs computed from annual distribution and used as basis for selecting loading distribution.
ESALCalc based: No directly usable annual axle distribution. ESALCalc estimate more reasonable than agency ESAL estimate and used as basis for selecting loading distribution.
Quantitative acceptance: Single available annual axle distribution quantitatively screened and accepted for direct use. No better than "Fair" quality.
Location-based - No axle data or agency provided ESAL estimates. Loading distributions selected based on location information only.
Better: Based on WIM data that satisfies accuracy requirements for ASTM E1318-09 Type I WIM systems passing data reasonableness checks. Data available for at least 1 year, not necessarily all months.
Multiple years of unknown data quality, with typical distribution accepted as representative.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Agency ESAL estimates are consistent with ESALs computed from distribution. Typical distribution accepted for use.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Agency ESAL estimates are reasonable but smaller than ESALs computed from distribution. Typical distribution accepted for use.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Agency ESAL estimates are reasonable but larger than with ESALs computed from distribution. Distributions selected based on estimated ESALs.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Agency ESAL estimates are not reasonable. Distributions selected based on ESALs computed from distributions.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Reasonable agency ESAL estimates. ESALs computed from distributions are lower than reasonable. Axle distributions selected based on agency ESAL estimates.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Reasonable agency ESAL estimates. ESALs computed from distributions are higher than reasonable. Axle distributions selected based on agency ESAL estimates.
Multiple years of data of unknown quality. Agency ESAL estimates and ESALs computed from distributions are higher than reasonable. Axle distributions selected based on location.
Good: Based on WIM data collected by the equipment that does not have a calibration record. Data quality cannot be quantified. Values based on annual data summaries passing QC checks.
Fair: Based on WIM data collected by the equipment that does not have a calibration record. Data quality cannot be quantified. Values based on selected annual data summaries passing QC checks.
Default-State Pattern: Available WIM data are of unknown quality and did not pass study descriptive checks for acceptable precision and bias.
Default-PLUG Pattern: Available WIM data are of unknown quality and did not pass study descriptive checks for acceptable precision and bias. Loading pattern used to identify LTPP PLUG defaults.
Default-No Pattern PLUG: No WIM or information about loading pattern and/or weights are available. LTPP PLUG default values were assigned using information other than site-specific weight data.
Default-No Pattern State: No WIM or information about loading pattern and/or weights are available. Default values based on the data from the other WIM sites in the same state.
Atypical Axle Type: Reported axle group is not typical for a given vehicle class. ALDF values are not recommended for development of the defaults.
Low Axle Count: Computed values are based on low axle count (less than 200 axles) and may not be representative of typical loading condition for a given site.
Small Axle Sample: Computed values are based on a low number of days with data and may not be representative of typical loading condition for a given site.
Vehicle class-axle combination not present in data set. Default picked using the low volume criterion for the class.
Not Applicable: Zero ALDF, vehicle class is not present.

Best - More than 75 percent of years used are monitored vehicle class data, AND no year-to-year percentage in any class changes by more than 15 percent in the period.
Better - More than 50 percent of years used are monitored vehicle class data, AND no year-to-year percentage change in any class > 15 percent AND no years have a large year-to-year change in AADTT.
Good - More than 50 percent, but < 75 percent of years used are monitored vehicle class data AND no year-to-year change in any class > 15 percent in less than 3 years.
Fair - More than 25 percent, but < 50 percent of years used are monitored vehicle class data OR at least one the year-to-year change in any truck class > 15 percent.
Poor - Less than 25 percent of the years used are monitored vehicle class data.
Bad - No monitoring data are present.
